title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs merge bases, topology, and integration strategies from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the merge topology and strategy decision record. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for merge topology and strategy decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Merge Base Multiple · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G17 is a review in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

Which action demonstrates Gita Merge Base Multiple without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.

stimulus.title: Gita Merge Result Defense case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Case G23 is a review in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A conflict-free merge proves both sides remain behaviorally compatible.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication. Artifact under review: merge topology and strategy decision record.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Merge Result Defense, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Gita Merge Result Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G24 is a review in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
